title: ICE Agents Shoot Man Through Car Window in Patterson, CA; Eyewitness Contradicts DHS Account
excerpt: ICE agents shot Carlos Ivan Mendoza Hernández through his car window along I-5 in Patterson, California. An eyewitness and dashcam video emerged days later corroborating the victim's account that shots began before he attempted to drive away, directly contradicting federal officials' justification.
content:
On April 7, 2026, multiple ICE agents fired into a vehicle near Interstate 5 in Patterson, California, wounding the driver. The incident was the latest in a string of ICE vehicle shootings documented since mid-2025.

An eyewitness and her attorney presented dashboard-camera footage showing at least one agent opened fire unprovoked, corroborating the shooting victim's account that shots began before he tried to drive away. Federal officials had not addressed the multiple contradictory accounts as of the date of publication.

  • A Wall Street Journal investigation had previously documented 13 similar incidents since July 2025 in which immigration agents fired at or into civilian vehicles.
  • The man was taken from the hospital directly into FBI custody after his release, according to his attorney.
  • The shooting followed the same pattern identified by Stateline and the WSJ: agents claiming self-defense while available footage raised questions about whether the threat was imminent.
mediaPlaceholder: Dashboard camera footage from eyewitness vehicle at scene of Patterson ICE shooting
